/.title: XcrowHub | Protected escrow for crypto P2P deals (NIM + USDT)
/.description: XcrowHub locks buyer funds in escrow until delivery is confirmed. Private deals have 0% fees and marketplace sales support bidding.
/.heading: Protected escrow for crypto P2P deals
/.summary: Create private deals or marketplace sales in NIM and USDT. Buyer funds stay protected until delivery is confirmed.

/marketplace.title: Crypto Escrow Marketplace with Bidding | XcrowHub
/marketplace.description: Browse XcrowHub marketplace listings, buy at the listed price or make an offer, and complete every sale through protected crypto escrow.
/marketplace.heading: Buy, sell and bid with escrow protection
/marketplace.summary: Public listings support buy-now payments and buyer offers. Each accepted sale becomes a protected escrow deal.

sections.heading: Buy now or make an offer
sections.body: A buyer can pay the listed price or submit a bid for the seller to accept, reject or counter.

sections.heading: Escrow on every sale
sections.body: Payment stays in a protected hold until the buyer confirms delivery or a dispute is resolved.

sections.heading: Clear marketplace fee
sections.body: Creating a listing is free. A 1% seller-side fee applies only when a marketplace sale completes.

/how-it-works.title: How XcrowHub Escrow Works | Protected Crypto Deals
/how-it-works.description: Learn how XcrowHub protects a crypto deal from creation and escrow payment through delivery, confirmation, payout and dispute resolution.
/how-it-works.heading: A protected deal from payment to payout
/how-it-works.summary: XcrowHub keeps the buyer's payment in escrow while the seller completes delivery. Both sides can track every stage.

sections.heading: 1. Create the deal
sections.body: Set the item, currency, price and delivery terms, then share a private link or publish a marketplace listing.

sections.heading: 2. Buyer funds escrow
sections.body: The buyer pays in NIM or USDT and both parties can see that the funds are protected.

sections.heading: 3. Seller delivers
sections.body: The seller completes the order, marks it delivered and can attach supporting proof.

sections.heading: 4. Confirm and release
sections.body: The buyer confirms delivery and the payout is released. A contested deal enters proof-based human review.

/docs.title: XcrowHub Documentation | Escrow, Payments & Disputes
/docs.description: Read the complete XcrowHub guide covering private deals, marketplace bidding, escrow payments, delivery, disputes, fees and referrals.
/docs.heading: The complete XcrowHub guide
/docs.summary: Understand private deals, marketplace orders, supported currencies, payment verification, disputes, fees and referrals.

sections.heading: Private and marketplace deals
sections.body: Choose a private shareable deal or a public reusable listing with buy-now and bidding options.

sections.heading: Payments and delivery
sections.body: Learn how NIM and USDT payments are verified and when escrowed funds can be released.

sections.heading: Disputes and security
sections.body: Review evidence windows, human dispute decisions, wallet authentication and account safety.

/privacy.title: Privacy Policy | XcrowHub
/privacy.description: Read how XcrowHub handles wallet identifiers, deal records, support messages, technical data and privacy choices.
/privacy.heading: XcrowHub privacy policy
/privacy.summary: This policy explains what information XcrowHub processes, why it is needed and the choices available to users.

sections.heading: Information used
sections.body: XcrowHub processes wallet identifiers, deal activity and information a user chooses to provide for support or disputes.

sections.heading: Why it is used
sections.body: Information is used to operate escrow, prevent abuse, resolve disputes and maintain service security.

/terms.title: Terms of Service | XcrowHub
/terms.description: Read the XcrowHub terms for escrow deals, marketplace listings, payments, delivery, disputes, fees and acceptable use.
/terms.heading: XcrowHub terms of service
/terms.summary: These terms describe the responsibilities of buyers and sellers when using XcrowHub protected deals.

sections.heading: Deal responsibilities
sections.body: Users must provide accurate terms, pay agreed amounts, deliver as promised and submit truthful evidence.

sections.heading: Escrow and disputes
sections.body: Funds follow the deal state and may be released, refunded or split after an evidence-based dispute decision.

/support.title: XcrowHub Support | Help with Escrow Deals
/support.description: Get XcrowHub help for deal payments, delivery, marketplace orders, disputes, wallet access and technical issues.
/support.heading: Help with your XcrowHub deal
/support.summary: Open the support area inside XcrowHub for account-specific help, or use the public guidance to prepare the details the team needs.

sections.heading: Deal and payment help
sections.body: Include the deal ID, payment transaction and a clear description of the issue.

sections.heading: Dispute evidence
sections.body: Keep delivery files, screenshots, messages and transaction hashes ready for the proof window.

sections.heading: Technical support
sections.body: Report the device, Nimiq Pay version, page and exact action that caused the problem.

/find.title: Find an XcrowHub Deal | Open Deal ID or Link
/find.description: Open an existing XcrowHub escrow deal using its deal ID, shared payment link or QR code in the web app or Nimiq Pay.
/find.heading: Open an existing protected deal
/find.summary: Use the deal ID or shared XcrowHub link to open the correct escrow status and payment screen.

sections.heading: Use a deal ID
sections.body: Paste the XcrowHub deal ID exactly as it appears on the receipt or shared link.

sections.heading: Open a shared link
sections.body: A seller can share a private deal link that opens the correct deal in the XcrowHub web app or Nimiq Pay.

sections.heading: Scan the QR code
sections.body: The XcrowHub scanner can read a deal QR code and take the buyer to the protected payment flow.
